evals.prompt: Scan Reddit for content ideas this week. I want to know what DevOps beginners are struggling with so I can make videos about it.
evals.expected_output: A pain-bank.md file at ~/Documents/DT Vault/1-projects/tiktok/pain-bank.md with pain signals organized by Big 5 categories, each with a one-liner pain statement, raw quote, source link, and recurrence count.

assertions.text: pain-bank.md has valid YAML frontmatter with title, type, tags, and permalink fields
At least 3 of the 5 Big 5 category sections (Cost/Price, Problems/Negatives, Versus/Comparisons, Reviews, Best-of Lists) contain at least one entry
Every entry has all required fields: pain statement (H3), Recurrence, Subreddit, Raw quote, Link, First seen, Fingerprint
Pain statements are under 15 words, written from the audience's perspective (use 'I' or 'my', not 'users' or 'people'), and capture emotional core
No HTML tags or entities (&, <, etc.) appear anywhere in pain-bank.md
All Link fields contain actual reddit.com URLs from the RSS feed, not example/placeholder URLs
Stdout includes a summary with new seeds count, recurrence bumps, and top pain this week

evals.prompt: Refresh the pain bank — I ran it last week and want to see what's new and what's recurring.
evals.expected_output: The existing pain-bank.md is updated: new entries added, recurrence counts incremented on matching topics, no duplicate entries created. Summary shows both new seeds and recurrence bumps.

assertions.text: No two entries share the same fingerprint — recurring topics increment the existing entry's recurrence count instead of creating a new entry
At least one entry has recurrence > 1, indicating it was seen in both runs
Entries with recurrence > 1 have an 'Also seen' field listing the additional sighting date(s)
The total number of entries is greater than the first run (new topics were found)
The 'Last harvested' date in the header matches today's date

evals.prompt: I need some fresh video topics for my TikTok. What are people in the DevOps world complaining about on Reddit lately?
evals.expected_output: The skill triggers on this indirect phrasing and produces a pain bank with classified content seeds, same as a direct invocation.

assertions.text: The content-seed-harvester skill was invoked (fetch-reddit.sh was run and pain-bank.md was written)
pain-bank.md follows the same format as eval 1 — Big 5 sections, proper entry fields, valid frontmatter
